sourceMovie.title: Selma
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Selma works as a history movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The history and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Lincoln
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Selma clicked for you, Lincoln (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in history and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Selma?
faq.answer: Try Lincoln, The Trial of the Chicago 7, Glory—each shares DNA with Selma while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Selma?
faq.answer: Selma (2014) is best described as History, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Selma worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this history lane.
